questions.question: What is the control sequence for execution of the instruction MUL R3, R1, R2 (R3 ← R1 × R2) including the instruction fetch phase using single bus architecture? Draw the block diagram for the necessary operation.

A set-associative cache memory consists of 128 blocks divided into four block sets. The main memory consists of 16,384 blocks and each block contains 256 eight-bit words. (a) How many bits are required for addressing the main memory? (b) How many bits are needed to represent the TAG, SET, and WORD fields? (c) Draw the diagram that represents the hit and miss.

A computer employs RAM chips of 128×8 and ROM chips of 512×8. The system needs 256 bytes of RAM, 1024×16 of ROM, and two interface units with 256 registers each. A memory-mapped I/O configuration is used. (a) Compute the total number of decoders needed. (b) Give the memory-address map for each of the memory chips.

A computer has a 1 MB memory with 64-bit word sizes. Each block of memory stores 16 words. The computer has a direct-mapped cache of 128 blocks. (a) What is the address format? (b) If we change the cache to a 4-way set-associative cache, what is the new address format? (c) Draw the diagram that represents the hit and miss.
Why does DMA have priority over the CPU when both request a memory transfer? With a suitable diagram, justify.
Demonstrate step-by-step multiplication using Booth's Multiplication Algorithm. A = +36 (Multiplicand), B = −34 (Multiplier). Use 7-bit registers.
Divide the following unsigned numbers using the Restoring Division Algorithm. Dividend = 47, Divisor = 12.
Find the floating point number for the given single precision IEEE-754 format: Sign: 0 | Exponent: 10000101 | Mantissa: 01010100100000000000000.

A computer employs RAM chips of 512×8 and ROM chips of 512×8. System needs 1024×8 bytes of RAM, 1024×16 of ROM, and two interface units with 1024 registers each. (a) Draw the memory address map for the system. (b) How many lines must be decoded for chip select? (c) Draw the memory chip connection diagram.
A digital computer has a memory unit of 128K and a cache memory of 1K words. Cache uses direct mapping with a block size of four words. How many bits are in the Tag, Cache line, and Block offset?
Consider a fully associative mapped cache of size 128 KB with block size 1024 bits. There are 17 bits in the tag. Find the size of main memory.
Consider a 4-way set associative mapped cache with block size 4 KB. Main memory size is 16 GB and there are 10 bits in the tag. Find the size of cache memory.

Consider the following page reference string: 9, 8, 5, 4, 5, 6, 7, 5, 4, 8, 9, 5, 4, 6, 7, 9, 4, 6, 3. Assuming 3 frames (initially loaded with 9, 8, 2), find page faults for: (a) LRU (b) FIFO (c) Optimal.

A program runs in 10 seconds on computer X with 2 GHz clock. Computer Y runs the same program in 6 seconds. (i) What is the number of CPU cycles on computer X? (ii) If Y requires 10% more cycles than X, what is the clock rate for Y? (iii) If Z runs with clock rate of Y and clock cycles of X, find CPU time of Z. (iv) Identify the fastest and slowest machine among X, Y, and Z. (v) Computer A executes 3 million instructions in the CPU time of the fastest computer. Determine MIPS.

A computer employs RAM chips of 1024×8 and ROM chips of 1024×8. System needs 2K×8 bytes of RAM, 2K×8 bytes of ROM, and four interface units with four registers each. Memory-mapped I/O; two highest-order bits: 00=RAM, 01=ROM, 10=Interface. (a) How many RAM and ROM chips are needed? (b) Draw a memory-address map. (c) Give hexadecimal address ranges for RAM, ROM, and Interface. (d) Develop a chip layout.

Consider a 2-way set associative cache memory with total 8 cache lines and main memory with 128 blocks. Cache is initially empty. Use Optimal replacement policy. Memory references: 5, 2, 3, 7, 3, 15, 14, 2, 1, 21, 13, 72, 12, 5, 19, 3, 2, 1, 22, 9, 26, 10, 5, 1, 7, 26, 14, 9, 19, 26. Compute Hit Ratio (H) and Miss Ratio (M). Which references remain in cache at the end?

A hard disk with a transfer rate of 10 MB/s uses DMA. Processor runs at 600 MHz, takes 300 cycles to initiate and 900 cycles to complete DMA. Transfer size = 20 KB. What is the % of processor time consumed?
Discuss the functionality of interfaces in the I/O subsystem with a neat diagram.
